sanskrit: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत। तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि॥
transliteration: dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhārata | tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śocitum arhasi ||
literal_meaning: The indweller (dehī) is eternally (nityam) unslayable (avadhyaḥ); this (ayam) applies to every body (dehe sarvasya). Therefore (tasmāt) you ought not (na arhasi) to grieve (śocitum) for any beings (sarvāṇi bhūtāni).
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s anticipatory grief by asserting an indestructible essence that transcends physical death, shifting the emotional focus from loss to duty.
psychological_state: Cognitive re-appraisal through existential reassurance; a move from egoic grief to dharma-based action.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon hesitating to operate on a loved one, paralyzed by the fear of causing death.
anchor_text: Nothing truly dies; do your duty without mourning the body.
